title: National Championship & Gala
intro: Each year the Moot Board of Governors brings its member societies together for a marquee weekend — a national championship moot followed by an awards banquet honouring the year in pre-law advocacy across Canada.
competitionText: A proposed national championship moot that would crown the top pre-law team in the country. Member societies would send their strongest two-person teams to argue a shared appellate problem before panels of legal professionals. This event is currently under consideration by the Board and depends on host capacity and funding.
banquetText: The championship weekend closes with a formal banquet celebrating the season's achievements. The Gala gathers representatives, competitors, and officials from every member society for dinner and the presentation of the Board's annual awards.
awardsIntro: Presented each year at the Governors' Gala to recognize excellence across the national pre-law community.

awards.title: Best-Run Competition
awards.description: For the member society that hosted the most outstanding moot of the year — logistics, judging, and competitor experience.

awards.title: Society of the Year
awards.description: For the member society that did the most to advance pre-law mooting and advocacy over the season.

awards.title: Most Accomplished Team
awards.description: For the two-person team with the strongest competitive record across the year's national moots.

awards.title: Best Oral Advocate
awards.description: For the top individual advocate across the season, as recognized by judging panels.

awards.title: Rising Society
awards.description: For an outstanding newcomer to the Board making an immediate mark on national mooting.

awards.title: Distinguished Service
awards.description: For exceptional contribution to the Board and the growth of pre-law advocacy in Canada.
